seo.title: AI for non-technical teams · role-adapted, EU AI Act Article 4 ready
seo.description: Role-adapted AI training for operations, HR, finance and customer support teams. Built on the Copilot, Gemini and ChatGPT licenses you already pay for. From 3,000 EUR.
seo.keywords: AI training for non-technical teams, EU AI Act Article 4 training, Microsoft Copilot training, ChatGPT training, Gemini Workspace training, AI literacy programme, AI workflow design, role-based AI training, Romania, EU
seo.courseDescription: Modular AI adoption programme for non-technical business teams. EU AI Act Article 4 ready. Discovery, Short Sprint or Full Programme tracks.
seo.docTitle: AI for Non-Technical People, The Unlearning School

hero.bannerAlt: Modern office workers learning AI tools
hero.title: Your team is already using AI. You just don't control how.
hero.subtitle: A structured adoption program for non-technical business teams, built entirely on the AI tools and licenses you already pay for. Modular: 1 session to full program.

challenges.title1: AI adoption without structure creates gaps in
challenges.intro: Across business teams, AI adoption often starts before leadership has a clear model for how it should be used. Some employees rely on it daily, others avoid it. No standards. No visibility. No accountability. With AI regulation tightening across Romania and Europe, this is no longer just inefficiency, it is legal and operational risk.

items.body: Since February 2025, the EU AI Act requires organisations to ensure AI literacy among employees. Article 4 is a legal obligation, not a recommendation.
Some people use AI daily, others barely use it at all. Output quality varies from person to person.
Teams save time in isolated ways, but not at a team level. Managers have little visibility into what is actually changing.
AI becomes personal experimentation, not an operational capability.
Unregulated AI use risks data leaks through unsupervised inputs and outputs.
Inconsistent tool usage makes compliance audits harder to pass.

challenges.footer1: Teams lose time on research, preparation, reporting, and repetitive tasks that AI could handle. Managers know the shift is happening, but often lack a structured framework for guiding adoption, setting expectations, and measuring progress.
challenges.footerEm: The cost of unstructured AI adoption is not just productivity loss. It is legal, operational, and reputational risk.

objective.body: Help your team stop experimenting with AI and start using it. Build a first working cohort of AI-enabled professionals, supported by managers who can guide adoption, reduce inconsistency, and turn scattered experimentation into structured execution.

items: Operations teams
Finance teams
HR teams

HR
L&D

audience.footer: Any department mix. Cross-functional cohorts welcome.

items.name: Discovery Session
items.body: Understand where AI fits, where it doesn't, and what's blocking adoption.

items.name: Short Sprint
items.body: Pick one operational bottleneck, build and test an AI workflow for it.

items.name: Full Program
items.body: Covers adoption infrastructure, workflow integration, internal champions, and a 30-day action plan.

entry.footer: Book the AI Adoption Call. Get your maturity score and roadmap. Scope the right format together. No commitment until you see the fit.

structure.intro: Each session builds on the previous one, creating a progressive learning journey that translates directly into day-to-day execution. Click any module to expand.

sessions.title: Where AI Fits in Your Operations
sessions.body: This session maps your team's actual workflows and identifies where AI creates value vs. where it doesn't.
topics: What AI can do beyond chatbot-style prompting
The foundations that actually matter for business teams
AI vs automation vs agents, without unnecessary jargon
What good AI usage looks like across different roles
Where human judgment remains critical
How the program will run and what is expected between sessions

practice.body: setting permanent rules for how your AI assistant responds (tone, format, context)
controlling what your AI remembers across conversations
disabling model training on your conversations; enterprise data boundaries
when to use fast/instant models vs deep-thinking models vs specialised models
matching the right AI tool to the right task based on capability, not brand

sessions.outcome: By the end of Session 1, the cohort has a shared language, a realistic scope, and concrete working scenarios. Each participant defines 1–2 real use cases to work on during the program.

sessions.title: Building Your First AI Workflows
sessions.body: This session focuses on practical AI usage inside real business workflows.
topics: How to choose the right tool for the right task
ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini vs Copilot in real business contexts
Research workflows for internal and external analysis
Using AI for document drafting, summarisation, and review
Structuring reports and operational materials faster
Reducing time spent on repetitive preparation tasks

practice.body: using extended research capabilities to generate comprehensive reports with cited sources in 10+ minutes
Clear Idea, Sequential Approach, Reverse Engineering, Cloning
adjusting output precision (low for compliance/finance) vs creative freedom (high for ideation)
uploading your own documents and interrogating them without external data leakage
conversational search that returns answers with verifiable references

sessions.outcome: By the end of Session 2, participants have tested practical workflows and built prompt frameworks they can immediately use in their role.

sessions.title: Team Adoption, Workflows, and Champions
sessions.body: This session focuses on operational workflows and the internal adoption layer.
topics: Using AI for process documentation, analysis, and operational planning
Competitor scanning and market synthesis
Faster first drafts without lowering quality standards
How managers create minimum standards for AI usage
Identifying 2 to 3 internal AI Champions
How to measure adoption beyond vanity metrics
How to reduce the gap between early adopters and the rest of the team

practice.body: middle managers caught between AI-fluent juniors and results-driven seniors
shifting from asking AI to write content, to having AI agents execute multi-step workflows autonomously
group-based learning delivers 85%+ completion vs 10-15% for self-paced courses
identifying 2-3 team members who become the go-to reference for AI adoption
measuring success by time recovered, not modules completed

sessions.outcome: By the end of Session 3, the team has both practical workflows and a clearer internal structure for sustained adoption.

sessions.title: Results, Refinement, and 30-Day Action Plan
sessions.body: This final session turns learning into visible business decisions.
topics: Presentation of tested use cases
Review of what worked and what did not
Feedback and refinement
Identifying repeatable workflows worth keeping
Deciding what should scale after the first cohort
Defining ownership, expectations, and next actions
Building a 30-day roadmap for ongoing adoption

practice.body: understanding that the same prompt generates different results, and how to iterate effectively
building functional web applications and tools using AI-assisted development platforms
providing an existing product as reference and asking AI to analyse its business model, audience, and structure
replicating the structure and style of an existing product as a starting point for a new project
post-program action plan with clear ownership, milestones, and accountability based on hours saved

sessions.outcome: By the end of Session 4, your team has a first trained cohort, visible implementation examples, and a practical next-step plan.

items: A first trained cohort across your teams
Validated prompt frameworks for real use cases
Tested AI workflows for research, reporting, operations, and decision support
2–3 internal champions identified
A manager-facing 30-day adoption roadmap
A clearer standard for how AI should and should not be used inside your teams
Clear ownership for next-step rollout

items: AI vendor & license audit of your current tool stack
Live sessions (scope agreed after initial meeting)
Cross-functional design for any department mix
Live practical exercises built on your existing AI tools
Role-relevant prompt frameworks for your team
A custom prompt kit adapted to your workflows
Tested workflow examples adapted to your context
Identification of internal champions
A manager-facing AI adoption roadmap
One 30-day follow-up session after delivery
Official certificate of completion from The Unlearning School

pricing.subtitle: Everything is fully customised to your team's context, tools, and existing AI licenses. Pricing scales with group size. Full customisation included in every tier.

pricing.altCta: Or book the AI Adoption Call first

confidence.body: If, after Session 1, your team concludes the program is not the right fit, the remaining sessions can be stopped and not invoiced.

bestForItems: Volume pricing agreed after initial meeting

selfTestEyebrow: Not sure if you need this?

items.body: Modular sessions. Dates agreed in advance with your team.

Everything is tailored. Prompt kit, exercises, and workflows are adapted to your actual context, and built on the AI tools and licenses you already use. Initial meeting used to tailor use cases and expected outcomes.

why.title1: Designed to change how the work gets done
why.intro: The program is delivered as modular sessions rather than a 2-day intensive. Lower operational disruption. Space to apply learning between sessions. Better retention, feedback, and refinement based on real usage.
why.footer1: The program is not designed to impress in the room.
why.footerEm: It is designed to change how the work gets done after the room.

items: Number of real use cases adopted by the teams
Number of workflows tested and retained
Consistency of AI usage across participants
Manager visibility into adoption and output quality
Existence of a practical 30-day plan owned internally
Identification of internal champions who can carry adoption further
success.footer: ROI can also be calibrated using your team's real data during the diagnostic stage, so the business case reflects actual workflows, time use, and internal realities, not only generic benchmarks.

leadership.bio1: 20+ years across innovation, digital transformation, executive education, entrepreneurship, and practical workforce training.
leadership.bio2: Worked across corporate, academic, public-sector, and entrepreneurial environments, designing and delivering learning experiences that translate into execution, not just awareness.
focusItems: Practical training for cross-functional business teams
Workshops, hackathons, and keynotes for fast proof and leadership buy-in
credentialsItems: Google Generative AI for Leaders (certification in progress)

items.body: A free 30-minute call that scores your AI adoption maturity, surfaces the gaps, and produces a ranked next-step roadmap. No commitment.

items.h: We scope the right format together
items.body: Based on the meeting, we agree on the right entry point: a single session, a short sprint, or the full program. Everything is adapted to your team's tools and context.
The program begins on your timeline. Modular delivery means you control the pace. From this initial engagement, we grow the number of trained participants together.

fundingBridge.titleStart: Now you know what to build.
fundingBridge.body: Prototypes built in the programme become production with budget - agents, integrations, Copilot and Azure licences, dev work. Vendor MDF, AWS co-sell and EU digital grants exist exactly for this. Most companies qualify.

cta.title: A program designed to fit your team. Not the other way around.
cta.subtitle: Pay and enrol your cohort, or book the AI Adoption Call first to score your maturity and get a ranked roadmap. No pitch deck, no generic workshop.
cta.note: Modular delivery · Built on your existing tools · Certificate of completion

faqList.question: Who is this programme for?
faqList.answer: Operations, finance, HR, customer support, and engineering teams that have access to AI tools but inconsistent or low usage.

faqList.question: Does it satisfy EU AI Act Article 4?
faqList.answer: Yes. The programme is designed to deliver auditable AI literacy required by EU AI Act Article 4, in force since February 2025.

faqList.question: Which AI tools do we use?
faqList.answer: The tools your organisation already pays for, typically Microsoft 365 Copilot, ChatGPT Enterprise, Gemini, or your internal assistants.

faqList.question: How much does it cost?
faqList.answer: 3,000 EUR for up to 5 participants, 5,000 EUR for up to 10 participants. Custom pricing for over 10. All tiers include the same content, audit, and certificate.

curriculumLabels.useCases: Real use cases participants work on
